title: elymus pungens subsp. campestris
intro: Elymus pungens subsp. campestris, commonly known as the tall wheat grass or blue wildrye, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Elymus pungens subsp. campestris include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health issues.
